content.full_text:
''

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 30, 1988

Dorothy Benker
25501 Sugar Pine Drive
Pioneer, California 95666

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 88-345

Dear Mrs. Benker:

Your August 23, 1988, letter to Attorney General John
Van de Kamp has been referred to the Fair Political Practices
Commission. You have requested advice concerning your disclosure
responsibilities under the conflict of interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

QUESTION

Are you obligated to file a Statement of Economic Interests
(Form 730) because of your position as a Commissioner on the Amador
County Airport Land Use Commission?

CONCLUSION

The conflict of interest code for the Amador County Airport
Land Use Commission is not yet in effect. Commissioners will be
designated in the conflict of interest code. Therefore, when the
code becomes effective you will be required to file a statement of
economic interests.

FACTS

The County of Amador is in the process of adopting a conflict
of interest code for the Amador County Airport Land Use Commission
which is wholly within the County of Amador. The conflict of
interest code for the Amador County Airport Land Use Commission has
not become effective yet. The Amador County Board of Supervisors is
the code reviewing body for this commission.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Dorothy Benker
September 30, 1988
Page 2


ANALYSIS

     Each state and local government agency is required to submit a
conflict of interest code for approval within six months after the
agency comes into existence. (Section 87303.) The proposed
conflict of interest code must be approved by the code reviewing
body. Review and approval of the code must be carried out under
procedures which guarantees to officers, employees, members and
consultants of the agency and to residents of the jurisdiction
adequate notice and a fair opportunity to present their views.
(Section 87311.) Conflict of interest codes become effective 30
days after approval. Initial Statements of Economic Interests (Form
730) must be filed no later than 30 days after the effective date of
the conflict of interest code. (Section 87302.)

     As a designated employee, you must file a statement of
economic interests pursuant to the disclosure category for your
position as set out in the conflict of interest code. On an
"initial" statement, you must disclose investments and interests in
real property of the type described in your disclosure category
owned directly, indirectly or beneficially by you, your spouse or
your dependent children if the interests are located in or doing
business in your jurisdiction.2/

     I hope this has been helpful to you. If you have any
additional questions, please call me at (916) 322-5662.

                                          very truly yours,

                                          Diane M. Griffiths
                                          General Counsel

                                          [illegible]
                                   By:    Alice Hughes
                                          Technical Assistance &
                                          Analysis Division

AAH:ssa


     2/ "Jurisdiction" means the district or other geographical
area in which the Amador County Airport Land Use Commission has
jurisdiction. Real property is "within the jurisdiction" if the
property is located in or within two miles of any land owned or used
by the Commission.

25501 Sugar Pine Drive
Pioneer, CA  95666

August 23, 1988


Attorney General John Van de Kamp
1515 K Street, #511
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear Sir:

I am a retired private citizen serving as a Commissioner
on the Amador County Airport Land Use Commission.

Amador County Counsel provided Commissioners with State of
California (FPPC) 1987-1988 Form 730 Statement of Economic
Interests forms to be filled out.

I consulted the Amador County Clerk for a Disclosure
Category and was informed there is nothing in place in the
County requiring my filing this form.

My economic interests are held jointly with my husband and
he refuses disclosure of our holdings, none of which are in
conflict with the airport.

My question is this.  Is filing a Statement of Economic
Interests a requirement of my serving as a Commissioner on
the Airport Land Use Commission?

I would appreciate an answer to my question as soon as
possible, and I thank you very much for your assistance in
this matter.

Sincerely yours,

Dorothy S. Benker

JOHN K. VAN DE KAMP
Attorney General

State of California
DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE

PO BOX 944255
SACRAMENTO 9244-2550
Public Inquiry Unit: (916) 322-3360

August 30, 1988

Toll Free—California Only:
800-952-5225

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804


CORRESPONDENCE RECEIVED FROM:

Dorothy Benker
25501 Sugar Pine Drive
Pioneer, CA 95666

We are forwarding the enclosed letter for whatever action you
deem appropriate.

The correspondent has been informed of our action. Please
contact this office if we can be of assistance to you. You may
reach me at (916) 324-5765.

Very truly yours,

JOHN K. VAN DE KAMP
Attorney General

PETER E. KINGREN, Analyst
Public Inquiry Unit

PEK:mec
Enclosure

JOHN K. VAN DE KAMP
Attorney General

State of California
DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE

P.O. BOX 944255
SACRAMENTO 94244-2550
Public Inquiry Unit: (916) 322-3360

August 30, 1988

Toll Free—California Only:
800-952-5225

Dorothy Benker
25501 Sugar Pine Drive
Pioneer, CA 95666


Dear Ms. Benker:

This is in response to your letter dated August 23, 1988.

This is to acknowledge and thank you for the information
contained in your letter to Attorney General Van de Kamp.

The following agency is in a much better position to render
assistance to you in this matter.

We are therefore forwarding your inquiry to:

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Please send all further correspondence regarding this matter to
the above address.

We are pleased we could be of assistance in this matter.

Very truly yours,

JOHN K. VAN DE KAMP
Attorney General

PETER E. KINGREN, Analyst
Public Inquiry Unit

PEK:mec

88-345

JOHN K. VAN DE KAMP
Attorney General

State of California
DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE
P.O. BOX 944255
SACRAMENTO 94244-2550

August 30, 1988

Toll Free—California Only:
800-952-5225

Sacramento, CA 95804

Dorothy Benker
25501 Sugar Pine Drive
Pioneer, CA 95666

We are forwarding the enclosed letter for whatever action you
deem appropriate.

The correspondent has been informed of our action. Please
contact this office if we can be of assistance to you. You may
reach me at (916) 324-5765.

Very truly yours,

JOHN K. VAN DE KAMP
Attorney General

PETER E. KINGREN, Analyst
Public Inquiry Unit

JOHN K. VAN DE KAMP

August 30, 1988

800-952-5225

Dorothy Benker
25501 Sugar Pine Drive
Pioneer, CA 95666


Dear Ms. Benker:

This is in response to your letter dated August 23, 1988.

This is to acknowledge and thank you for the information
contained in your letter to Attorney General Van de Kamp.

The following agency is in a much better position to render

We are therefore forwarding your inquiry to:

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Please send all further correspondence regarding this matter to
the above address.

We are pleased we could be of assistance in this matter.

Very truly yours,

JOHN K. VAN DE KAMP
Attorney General

PETER E. KINGREN, Analyst
Public Inquiry Unit

PEK:mec

25501 Sugar Pine Drive
Pioneer, CA  95666

August 23, 1988


Attorney General John Van de Kamp
1515 K Street, #511
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear Sir:

I am a retired private citizen serving as a Commissioner
on the Amador County Airport Land Use Commission.

Amador County Counsel provided Commissioners with State of
California (FPPC) 1987-1988 Form 730 Statement of Economic
Interests forms to be filled out.

I consulted the Amador County Clerk for a Disclosure
Category and was informed there is nothing in place in the
County requiring my filing this form.

My economic interests are held jointly with my husband and
he refuses disclosure of our holdings, none of which are in
conflict with the airport.

My question is this.  Is filing a Statement of Economic
Interests a requirement of my serving as a Commissioner on
the Airport Land Use Commission?

I would appreciate an answer to my question as soon as
possible, and I thank you very much for your assistance in
this matter.

Sincerely yours,

[signature]
Dorothy P. Benker

b

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 6, 1988


Dorothy Benker
25501 Sugar Pine Drive
Pioneer, CA  95666

Re:  88-345

Dear Ms. Benker:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on August 31, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission.  If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days of your request seeks formal written
advice.  If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to the information needed.  If your request is
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we
can.  (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                   Very truly yours,


                                   [signature]
                                   Jeanne Pritchard
                                   Chief
                                   Technical Assistance and Analysis
                                   Division

commissioners will be
